title: Selaginella nana
taxonomy: Selaginella nana is classified within the family Selaginellaceae, which is a group of vascular plants commonly known as spikemosses. This plant belongs to the division Lycopodiophyta, a group of ancient and primitive vascular plants. The family Selaginellaceae is further classified within the class Lycopodiopsida, which includes other lycopod families such as Huperziaceae and Lycopodiaceae. At the order level, Selaginella nana is placed within Selaginellales, a group of spikemosses that are characterized by their simple and linear stems. Overall, the classification of Selaginella nana reflects its unique position within the plant kingdom.
uses: Selaginella nana is known for its traditional medicinal uses in various cultures. In some parts of the world, the plant is used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. It is also believed to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help alleviate conditions like arthritis and other inflammatory diseases. Additionally, the plant is sometimes used to treat digestive problems, including diarrhea and constipation. However, further research is needed to confirm the effectiveness of Selaginella nana as a medicinal treatment.

preparations: Selaginella nana herbal preparations are not well-documented, but they are often used in traditional medicine due to their potential health benefits.
